chapter_title: Regul. by Tex. Dept. of Transp.
text:
A railway company doing business in this state is liable to the state for a penalty of not more than $5,000 each time the railway company:

(1) violates any provision of this subtitle or Subtitle D or fails or refuses to perform any duty imposed upon it for which a penalty has not been provided by law; or

(2) fails, neglects, or refuses to obey any requirement, order, judgment, or decree of the department.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 5
### Chapter 111. Regul. by Tex. Dept. of Transp.
##### § 111.057 — Penalty Not Otherwise Provided

A railway company doing business in this state is liable to the state for a penalty of not more than $5,000 each time the railway company:

    (1) violates any provision of this subtitle or Subtitle D or fails or refuses to perform any duty imposed upon it for which a penalty has not been provided by law; or

    (2) fails, neglects, or refuses to obey any requirement, order, judgment, or decree of the department.

*Added by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 85 (S.B.1540), Sec. 2.03, eff. April 1, 2011.*

subsections.text: A railway company doing business in this state is liable to the state for a penalty of not more than $5,000 each time the railway company:
violates any provision of this subtitle or Subtitle D or fails or refuses to perform any duty imposed upon it for which a penalty has not been provided by law; or
fails, neglects, or refuses to obey any requirement, order, judgment, or decree of the department.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 85 (S.B.1540), Sec. 2.03, eff. April 1, 2011.
